text:
I start a new react router v7 project I want to add shadcn To add `shadcn` (ShadCN UI) to your new React Router v7 project, you can follow these steps. ShadCN is a component library built on top of Radix UI primitives, styled with Tailwind CSS. It provides pre-built components that are customizable and accessible. HereŌĆÖs how you can integrate ShadCN into your React Router v7 project: --- ### 1. **Set Up Your React Router v7 Project** If you haven't already created a React Router v7 project, you can do so with the following commands: ```bash npx create-react-app my-app cd my-app npm install react-router-dom ``` --- ### 2. **Install Tailwind CSS** ShadCN uses Tailwind CSS for styling. Follow these steps to set up Tailwind CSS in your project: #### Install Tailwind CSS ```bash npm install -D tailwindcss postcss autoprefixer npx tailwindcss init ``` #### Configure `tailwind.config.js` Update your `tailwind.config.js` file to include the paths to your components: ```javascript /** @type {import('tailwindcss').Config} */ module.exports = { content: [ "./src/**/*.{js,jsx,ts,tsx}", // Add this line to scan your React files ], theme: { extend: {}, }, plugins: [], }; ``` #### Add Tailwind Directives to CSS Create a `src/index.css` file (or update it if it already exists) and add the following: ```css @tailwind base; @tailwind components; @tailwind utilities; ``` #### Import Tailwind CSS in Your App Make sure to import the `index.css` file in your `src/index.js` or `src/main.jsx`: ```javascript import './index.css'; ``` --- ### 3. **Install ShadCN** ShadCN provides a CLI tool to install and manage components. Install the CLI globally: ```bash npm install -g shadcn-ui ``` --- ### 4. **Initialize ShadCN** Run the following command to initialize ShadCN in your project: ```bash npx shadcn-ui init ``` This will set up ShadCN in your project and configure it to work with Tailwind CSS. --- ### 5. **Add Components** You can now add ShadCN components to your project. For example, to add a button component: ```bash npx shadcn-ui add button ``` This will generate a `Button` component in your project, typically under a `components` directory. --- ### 6. **Use ShadCN Components** You can now use the ShadCN components in your React Router v7 project. For example, in your `App.js`: ```javascript import { Button } from './components/Button'; function App() { return (

Welcome to My App

); } export default App; ``` --- ### 7. **Set Up React Router** If youŌĆÖre using React Router v7, set up your routes in `App.js` or wherever you define your routes: ```javascript import { BrowserRouter as Router, Routes, Route } from 'react-router-dom'; import { Button } from './components/Button'; function Home() { return (

Home Page

); } function About() { return

About Page

; } function App() { return ( } /> } /> ); } export default App; ``` --- ### 8. **Customize Components** ShadCN components are fully customizable. You can edit the generated components in the `components` directory to fit your design needs. --- ### 9. **Install Additional Plugins (Optional)** ShadCN components often use Tailwind plugins for advanced features. If a component requires a specific plugin, ShadCN will notify you during installation. For example, you might need the `@tailwindcss/forms` plugin for form components: ```bash npm install -D @tailwindcss/forms ``` Update your `tailwind.config.js` to include the plugin: ```javascript plugins: [require('@tailwindcss/forms')], ``` --- ### 10. **Start Your Development Server** Run your development server to see everything in action: ```bash npm start ``` --- ### Summary YouŌĆÖve now set up a React Router v7 project with ShadCN and Tailwind CSS. You can continue adding and customizing components as needed. ShadCNŌĆÖs CLI makes it easy to manage components, and its integration with Tailwind CSS ensures a seamless development experience.
